ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "title_of_courtesy" TEXT /* Title of courtesy (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.) */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the employee */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of residence */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of residence */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of residence */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of residence */,
  "home_phone" TEXT /* Home phone number of the employee */,
  "extension" TEXT /* Phone extension of the employee */,
  "photo" TEXT /* Photo of the employee */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager of the department */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department associated with the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_departments" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the employee's assignment to the department */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the employee's assignment to the department */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Whether this is the employee's primary department assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supervisors" (
  "supervisor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supervisor */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who is the supervisor */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the supervisor's term */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the supervisor's term */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supervisor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "benefit_name" TEXT /* Name of the benefit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the benefit */,
  "provider" TEXT /* Provider of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_benefits" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benefit */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the employee's enrollment in the benefit */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the employee's enrollment in the benefit */,
  "coverage_level" TEXT /* Level of coverage (e.g., individual, family) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payroll" (
  "payroll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payroll record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee */,
  "pay_frequency" TEXT /* Pay frequency of the employee */,
  "pay_rate" TEXT /* Pay rate of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payroll_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_off" (
  "time_off_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time off request */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the time off request */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the time off request */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of time off request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the time off request (e.g., approved, denied, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_off_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating given during the performance review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments given during the performance review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date of the training session */,
  "training_topic" TEXT /* Topic of the training session */,
  "trainer" TEXT /* Name of the trainer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "certifications" (
  "certification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each certification */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "certification_name" TEXT /* Name of the certification */,
  "certification_date" TEXT /* Date the certification was obtained */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the certification expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("certification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Degree obtained */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study */,
  "university" TEXT /* University attended */,
  "graduation_date" TEXT /* Date of graduation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skills" (
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "skill_name" TEXT /* Name of the skill */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of proficiency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "award_date" TEXT /* Date the award was received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disciplinary_actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disciplinary action */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the disciplinary action was taken */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the disciplinary action */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the disciplinary action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grievances" (
  "grievance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grievance */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "grievance_date" TEXT /* Date the grievance was filed */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the grievance */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the grievance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grievance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user account */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who owns the account */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the account */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, Manager, Employee) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role that has the permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user account */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id", "permission_id")
)
